doctor_turn: What brings you in?
patient_turn: Hi, yes I just came in today because I've been a bit concerned about some breathing difficulties over the last few days. It all started out with like a cough over the last week, but now I'm having trouble breathing as well.

doctor_turn: Oh OK, well I'm sorry to hear that. So you said a cough as well, so the cough at least a week back and the shortness of breath or difficulty breathing, when did that start?
patient_turn: So yeah, the cough, I usually have a cough. I've had COPD for a long time, but this past week the coughing has been much worse with a lot of more sputum and. Other than that, it started I would say two to three days ago. The cough got worse and then every time I'm moving around I like have to sit down and catch my breath, which is pretty abnormal for me.

doctor_turn: OK yeah, so alright. So let's focus on the cough first. You said it's a wet cough?
patient_turn: Yes, I am producing a lot of sputum.

doctor_turn: OK, what color is your sputum?
patient_turn: It's mainly like clear white.

doctor_turn: OK yeah so you don't see yellow pus or anything green or bloody in there?
patient_turn: No, I don't think so. But there's definitely like a lot more than what I usually produce.

doctor_turn: OK, umm this cough, does it sound harsh, barky or does it sound muffled?
patient_turn: I would say it's a bit harsh at times. I wouldn't say it's barky or muffled. I do find myself wheezing a little over the last few days as well.

doctor_turn: OK. Do you find you have any chest pain associated with the shortness of breath?
patient_turn: Oh no, I don't really have any pains.

doctor_turn: OK, and have you ever experienced anything like this before?
patient_turn: I did a couple of years ago, I had to be admitted because I had some shortness of breath.

doctor_turn: OK.
patient_turn: But that was, I think, two years ago.

doctor_turn: OK. And when you were admitted two years ago, did they tell you what you had?
patient_turn: Yeah, they said I had some kind of lung infection at that time, so they gave me some antibiotics while I was in the hospital.

doctor_turn: OK, did they ever say to you that you had anything else like asthma, COPD, any longstanding conditions?
patient_turn: Yeah so I've had COPD for more than... I guess more than 10 years now so I think it just got a lot worse with the infection.

doctor_turn: OK yeah alright. So this time around, have you had any fevers?
patient_turn: I've been having a couple of chills but like they would come and go. I didn't really think it was a fever.

doctor_turn: OK, any weight loss?
patient_turn: No.

doctor_turn: OK, how about changes in your hair or nails like or or skin like rashes?
patient_turn: Umm, no.

doctor_turn: Any changes in your vision or hearing?
patient_turn: Nope.

doctor_turn: And how about smell or taste?
patient_turn: Umm, no. I think they're the same.

doctor_turn: OK. Have a stuffy nose or runny nose?
patient_turn: I do feel a bit stuffy, but it's not too bad.

doctor_turn: OK. And any swelling in your in your legs or upper limbs?
patient_turn: No, not that I know.

doctor_turn: How about calf pain?
patient_turn: Nope.

doctor_turn: Dizziness or fainting spells?
patient_turn: No.

doctor_turn: Headaches?
patient_turn: No headaches.

doctor_turn: Memory loss or confusion?
patient_turn: Nope.

doctor_turn: Changes in your bowel movement or abdominal pain?
patient_turn: Nope.

doctor_turn: Changes to urinating?
patient_turn: No, that's all fine.

doctor_turn: OK, so besides the COPD, do you have any other longstanding conditions?
patient_turn: I do have some high blood pressure which I take, I think I take one of these medications called metoprolol.

doctor_turn: OK, and when was the last time you had your blood pressure checked?
patient_turn: Umm, probably like a month ago and it was pretty good. I've been checking by myself at home too and it hasn't been changing too much. I think the last one I took was probably like 135 / 80.

doctor_turn: Oh, I see. OK, any hospitalizations besides the one that you had for COPD a year back?
patient_turn: Umm, back when I was a kid I had my tonsils removed.

doctor_turn: OK, are you on any medications besides the metoprolol?
patient_turn: I do take multivitamins. I think that's yeah, that's about it.

doctor_turn: Do you take any puffers for your COPD?
patient_turn: Yeah, so I do have three like different puffers I take. I can't remember the name. I do have some puffers I don't take. I've been using them right now. It has been helping with some of my shortness of breath, but I've definitely been having to use them way more than I used to.

doctor_turn: OK. Do you know which puffer you're on?
patient_turn: Yeah, so I think one of them is called ventolin. OK, and then I think the second one is called the Spiriva.

doctor_turn: Just wanna make sure that you have one with a long acting and then the other one is a long acting beta agonist. OK, good. Alright, are you up to date with all your immunizations?
patient_turn: So one of them should be a short acting beta agonist.

doctor_turn: And are you allergic to anything?
patient_turn: Yeah, I think so.

doctor_turn: Any family history of lung conditions?
patient_turn: No, not that I know.

doctor_turn: OK. How about heart conditions?
patient_turn: Umm, no I don't think so. Yeah, no, I don't think so.

doctor_turn: OK. Alright, any cancers?
patient_turn: Yeah, my father died of a heart attack in his 60s.

doctor_turn: OK. Do you know what type of skin cancer like melanoma, basal cell, squamous cell?
patient_turn: My uncle had skin cancer.

doctor_turn: Oh I see. OK, OK, that's good. Do you smoke?
patient_turn: No, I think it wasn't the bad one. It was like the one they can remove right away.

doctor_turn: OK, how much do you smoke?
patient_turn: Yeah, I've been smoking for around, I would say the last 20 years.

doctor_turn: Umm, have you ever thought about quitting?
patient_turn: Probably I would say pack a day for the last 20 years.

doctor_turn: OK, yeah no problem. Do you drink alcohol?
patient_turn: Yeah I did. I did once and then went back onto it. I definitely do smoke less than before though. OK, but yeah, I don't think I'm ready to quit yet.

doctor_turn: OK, sure, that's fine. And any other drugs?
patient_turn: Umm, yeah, maybe once a week I would have a small Cup of whiskey.

doctor_turn: OK, and where do you live right now?
patient_turn: No, no marijuana, no other drugs.

doctor_turn: OK, any reasons to believe there are carpets, dust, mold, asbestos, anything that could be triggering your COPD?
patient_turn: Right now I live in a small bungalow in London.

doctor_turn: OK, have you had any contact with anyone that's been sick recently?
patient_turn: Umm, I don't think so. Everything is pretty up to date.

doctor_turn: OK, how about travel to anywhere?
patient_turn: No, I've been mostly trying to isolate and like just be in my own home.

doctor_turn: Yeah OK, that makes sense. Well, those were all the questions that I had for you today. Did you have any questions of your own?
patient_turn: Nope, I've been in Ontario since the beginning of the pandemic.
